verdict_reasoning: FN1 (fibronectin) is a secreted glycoprotein with no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no inner- or outer-leaflet lipidation of its own. It exists in two main forms: soluble plasma fibronectin (secreted by hepatocytes into blood) and cellular fibronectin (secreted and deposited as insoluble fibrils into the extracellular matrix). Its cell-surface association is entirely via reversible non-covalent binding to integrin receptors (especially α5β1) and heparan-sulfate proteoglycans — it is in equilibrium with the soluble pool and washes off in normal buffer. The recruiter is the surface integrin, not fibronectin itself. Pre-no checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed antibody/ADC/CAR-T clinical program targeting the FN1 protein body on the cell surface (programs targeting it address the ECM/stromal context or circulating form). (2) No shedding: FN1 is secreted whole, not a TM precursor whose ectodomain is shed. (3) Covalent cross-linking by transglutaminase (F13A, TGM2) deposits FN into the extracellular matrix/fibrin clot stroma, not onto a TM partner on the cell surface — this is matrix deposition, excluded per cardinal rule. (4) No documented PM minority pool with its own anchor. (5) Contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death outer-leaflet display distinct from ECM deposition; not restricted to a narrow germline/developmental lineage; not a lysosomal TM protein; no PM cycling pool; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. FN1 is therefore secreted-only with respect to surface accessibility.
